title: Caleana alcockii
taxonomy: Caleana alcockii is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Caleana, which is a part of the subtribe Caladeniinae. The subtribe Caladeniinae is further classified under the tribe Diurideae. The tribe Diurideae is a part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is a member of the family Orchidaceae.

intro: Caleana alcockii, also known as the alcock's spider orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Caleana alcockii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its soothing and protective effects.
